title: The Serpent in the Garden: Snakes in Religious Lore
highlights:
## Hindu Serpent Deities: Nagas and Cosmic Support

Nagas. Image by Openverse. Hindu mythology features some of the most complex and nuanced serpent symbolism in world religions. Nagas – divine snake beings – occupy an important place in Hindu cosmology as guardians of treasures and bringers of fertility. Shesha (or Ananta), the thousand-headed cosmic serpent, serves as Lord Vishnu’s resting place as he dreams the universe into being. In this role, the serpent represents the foundation of existence itself. The fearsome Vasuki allowed himself to  ...    the Genesis serpent. Some Gnostic texts portray the serpent not as a deceiver but as a liberator who freed humanity  ...    religious structures.
